title: Read, Unread, Star, Tag, Bulk Action, and Conflict Semantics · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Personal State Ownership
summary: Reading an entry mutates global source state, bulk operations partially apply silently, device updates overwrite each other, tags leak across tenants, and unread counts drift. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go personal state ownership increment in consistent personal reader-state model. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Keep read, unread, starred, hidden, saved, and tag relationships scoped to principal, tenant, and entry without mutating shared content.
Support narrow reflow, zoom, reduced motion, non-color state, image alternatives, content warnings, and user-controlled media.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Personal State Ownership · Theory Feed Go Personal State Ow…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 3157 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove private reader state, desired-state commands, bulk semantics, conflicts, and count repair; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
